basicDetails.implementingAgency: Tribal Affairs Department, Government Of Madhya Pradesh

nodalDepartmentName.label: Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md:
The scheme &quot;Cycle Distribution Scheme&quot; is implemented by the Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to provide cycles to village girls students to continue their education. Under this scheme, tribal girl students in Class 11th who travel more than 2 km to reach school and did not receive a bicycle in Class 9th are given a cash amount equivalent to the cost of a bicycle.
**Objective:**
The objective of the scheme is to reduce the dropout rate and promote the continuation of education for scheduled tribe girls in higher secondary schools. It aims to ensure that all scheduled tribe girls in the state continue their education beyond class 10, even if they need to enroll in higher secondary schools located in other villages.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - Under this scheme, a cash amount equal to the cost of the cycle credited directly to the bank account of the applicant.
schemeContent.briefDescription: Under this scheme, tribal girl students in Class 11th who travel more than 2 km to reach school and did not receive a bicycle in Class 9th are given a cash amount equivalent to the cost of a bicycle.

children.text: The scheme "Cycle Distribution Scheme" is implemented by the Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to provide cycles to village girls students to continue their education. Under this scheme, tribal girl students in Class 11th who travel more than 2 km to reach school and did not receive a bicycle in Class 9th are given a cash amount equivalent to the cost of a bicycle.

The objective of the scheme is to reduce the dropout rate and promote the continuation of education for scheduled tribe girls in higher secondary schools. It aims to ensure that all scheduled tribe girls in the state continue their education beyond class 10, even if they need to enroll in higher secondary schools located in other villages.
Under this scheme, a cash amount equal to the cost of the cycle credited directly to the bank account of the applicant.

To avail of the benefits under this scheme, eligible girl students can contact the District Convener/Assistant Commissioner, the District Office of the Tribal Welfare Department, or the Principal of the concerned school/institution.
The District Collector has the right to approve financial assistance to eligible girl students under the cycle supply scheme.

applicationProcess.process_md:
- To avail of the benefits under this scheme, eligible girl students can contact the District Convener/Assistant Commissioner, the District Office of the Tribal Welfare Department, or the Principal of the concerned school/institution.

**Note:** The District Collector has the right to approve financial assistance to eligible girl students under the cycle supply scheme.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The applicant should be a girl student.
   1. The girl student should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
   1. The girl student should be studying in Class 11.
   1. The girl student should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category.
   1. The girl student who did not receive a cycle in Class 9 is eligible under the scheme.
   1. The girl student should travel more than 2 km to reach her school.
   1. There is no restriction on the annual family income of the student.

children.text: The applicant should be a girl student.
The girl student should be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
The girl student should be studying in Class 11.
The girl student should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category.
The girl student who did not receive a cycle in Class 9 is eligible under the scheme.
The girl student should travel more than 2 km to reach her school.
There is no restriction on the annual family income of the student.
